codeReview.summary: All 8 required public methods are implemented and working. Events are emitted for TaskCreated, AgentStarted, and AgentFinished. However, TaskStateChanged event defined in OrchestratorEvent enum is never emitted - this is a non-blocking gap as the core functionality works.

issues.description: OrchestratorEvent::TaskStateChanged is defined but never emitted. The enum variant exists (line 196) but no code calls event_sender.send(OrchestratorEvent::TaskStateChanged {...}). State changes are reflected in task state but no event is emitted for observers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Orchestrator implementation is in lib.rs rather than a separate orchestrator.rs file as the task prompt suggested. This is acceptable - the module structure follows Rust conventions where a crate's main struct is often in lib.rs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-orchestrator/src/lib.rs contains pub struct Orchestrator and impl Orchestrator blocks

summary: Feature orch-orchestrator-api passes review. All 8 required methods (create_task, get_task, register_agent, assign_task, release_agent, start_task, start_validation, complete_task) are implemented and have test coverage. Events are emitted for task creation and agent lifecycle. One non-blocking issue: TaskStateChanged event is defined but not emitted.
